g.text: Long-running MCP server daemon with core infrastructure and opt-in modules. Two-layer anatomy: core ring (always present) + modules ring (opt-in per butler).
tracks tool calls per runtime session
MODULES RING — opt-in per butler (register_tools / migrations / on_startup / on_shutdown)
register_tools(mcp, config, db) — add MCP tools to FastMCP server
migration_revisions() — Alembic branch label for module migrations
on_startup(config, db, credential_store) — async init after dependency resolution
on_shutdown() — async cleanup during butler shutdown
tool_metadata() — sensitivity metadata (ToolMeta) per tool name
1. Trigger arrives (external MCP call or scheduler due task)
2. Spawner generates ephemeral MCP config (locked to this butler)
4. LLM CLI calls butler MCP tools, runs skills, returns
5. Session logged to core/sessions.py, result returned
MCP TOOL REGISTRATION FLOW (FastMCP SSE/Streamable per butler)
